questions.question: What is the main goal of Heap Sort?
choices: To arrange items based on importance

questions.question: Before sorting begins, what must be created first?
In Heap Sort, which item is placed at the top of the structure?
Why is the most important item placed at the top?
choices: To make it easy to remove first

questions.question: What happens after the top item is removed?
Why must the remaining items rearrange after removing one?
choices: To keep the correct order of importance

questions.question: How are items placed into their final order?
What happens to the group of items as sorting continues?
When does Heap Sort finish?
choices: When all items are placed in order

questions.question: Why is Heap Sort considered step-by-step?
choices: Because it places one correct item at a time

questions.question: What decides which item is handled first in Heap Sort?
If the structure is not rearranged properly after removing the top item, what could happen?
What makes Heap Sort different from simple sorting methods?
choices: It uses importance to organize items

questions.question: Why can’t all items be placed directly into final order at the start?
choices: Because they must first be organized by importance
Because the list is too long

questions.question: What happens every time the most important item is removed?
Why is organizing by importance helpful in sorting?
choices: It removes the need to compare
It ensures the correct item is chosen each time

questions.question: What is repeated during Heap Sort?
choices: Removing the top item and rearranging

questions.question: As more items are placed correctly, what happens to the unsorted group?
Why is Heap Sort reliable?
choices: Because it follows clear rules each step

questions.question: What is the most important idea to remember about Heap Sort?
choices: Organizing by importance leads to correct order
